mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and positive equity market sentiment, driving S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports corporate profits
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in economic activity and negative corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a triggered UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic growth and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High rates and inflation signal tight policy
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust industrial production growth (INDPRO) indicates healthy manufacturing activity, which supports corporate profits and drives positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output correlates with economic health
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an easing monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs for companies and consumers, stimulating economic activity and supporting equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25 percentage points over 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following a triggered FEDFUNDS decrease.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based consumer and investment confidence, driving economic growth and positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 1% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and retail are key demand indicators
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing productive capacity and output, which is a fundamental driver of corporate earnings and overall economic health, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output is a proxy for supply-side strength
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive.
